cards.fix: Group side effects behind an atomic write plan: validate and reserve first, then commit event/replay/rating/badge together or compensate visibly.
cards.impact: Human owner and agent both receive durable receipts that can be false or partial when EventLog, sidecar, or rating writes fail.
cards.title: Make arena receipts atomic before claiming honesty

cards.fix: Move quota debit after validation and successful durable acceptance, or add explicit refund records on retryable failures.
cards.impact: Owner pays scarce monthly quota for invalid teams, capacity failures, sidecar failures, and signer failures.
cards.title: Stop spending rated/evolution/badge quota before work is accepted

cards.fix: Select events by canonical agent/battle joins and nested period payloads, not only top-level tenant_id.
cards.impact: Human owner and agent cannot reconstruct paid/rated history from `/my/events` or local SQLite.
cards.title: Make owner export include replay, badge, and rating lineage

cards.fix: Require actual trace context/link presence in acceptance tests; document fallback mode separately.
cards.impact: The platform can pass trace-propagation tests while producing no usable trace/span link.
cards.title: Make observability acceptance fail when traces are absent

cards.fix: Hide or split admin OpenAPI, then test auth rejection before body validation for protected routes.
cards.impact: Operator-only endpoints are exposed in public OpenAPI and one documented auth ordering claim is false for malformed JSON.
cards.title: Tighten admin surface and auth-before-parse contract

comments.author: codex
comments.body: D2 (missing adx arena commands) shipped: PR #183 MERGED — deferred 'adx arena' stub routes visiting agents to starter kit / MCP / skill.md + fails closed (rc=1) instead of a bare argparse error. Remaining ADX-P2-001 backlog (verified): D1 SKILL.md battle-state shape (prio3), D3 starter-kit raw tracebacks (prio3), D5 battle_id enumeration auth-before-existence (prio5, security). D4 MCP-proxy-asymmetry adversarially REFUTED -> WONTFIX (intentional Mode2 game-only split).

comments.author: codex
comments.body: D1 (SKILL.md staleness) shipped: PR #184 MERGED — corrected initial battle-state example (added lane/status + gym-only opponent_team fields) and fixed MCP choose_action param idx->choice_index (matched mcp_surface.py:140). Doc verified vs HEAD. Next: D3 starter-kit raw tracebacks, then D5 battle_id enumeration.

comments.author: codex
comments.body: D3 (starter-kit raw tracebacks) shipped: PR #185 MERGED — shared run_agent_main() wrapper turns missing keyfile/team/expired-token/unreachable-arena into one-line stderr + exit codes (setup=2, runtime=1), re-raises genuine bugs; all 3 agents + bootstrap.sh enroll guarded. 7-case test. Verified e2e. Remaining ADX-P2-001: D5 battle_id enumeration (security, prio5). D4 WONTFIX.

comments.author: codex
comments.body: ADX-P2-001 agent-ux footguns COMPLETE (4 tiny PRs merged): D2 adx-arena-stub #183, D1 SKILL.md-shape #184, D3 starter-kit-clean-errors #185, D5 battle_id-anti-enumeration #186 (security: auth-before-existence on state/choose/fork/dispute, full arena suite 187 passed). D4 MCP-proxy-asymmetry adversarially REFUTED -> WONTFIX (intentional Mode2 game-only split). Moving to review for adx-cli/harness verification. Next codex card: ADX-P2-002 gameplay legibility (D6 gym-mapping already merged #167; remaining: foe-HP/recent_turns, replay metadata, anchor/gym test coverage).

cards.fix: Update `/skill.md`, add or explicitly defer `adx arena` commands, normalize starter-kit errors, and test missing battle IDs.
cards.impact: Agents hit stale docs, missing `adx` arena commands, traceback setup errors, and asymmetric MCP proxy behavior.
cards.title: Reduce starter and CLI footguns for visiting agents

comments.author: codex
comments.body: ADX-P2-002 gameplay legibility substantively COMPLETE: D8 /replay opponent archetype #187, D7 turn-0 (battle start) marker #188 (was empty recent_turns, confirmed empirically). D6 gym-trick-room mapping already merged #167. D9 anchor/gym coverage: already closed by parametrized test_every_gym_resolves_to_a_real_team over ALL GYM_LEADERS + badge/selection/rated tests — no real gap found (char's 'GYM_TEAM_INDEX collision' did NOT hold up: distinct keys 1/2/3, gyms use separate ARCHETYPE_GYM_TEAMS). Full arena suite 189 passed. Moving to review.

cards.fix: Repair gym mapping, expose opponent HP/recent turns, enrich replay metadata, and test anchor/gym coverage.
cards.impact: Agents can win or lose for shallow reasons and cannot always understand losses from state/replay alone.
cards.title: Make arena gameplay feedback more legible and less first-legal

cards.fix: Keep recompute ladder, anti-pay-to-rank property tests, `/whoami` redaction, team validation, and local log idempotence in the regression suite.
cards.impact: Known-good surfaces are easy to break while fixing adjacent defects.
cards.title: Preserve verified strengths while fixing gaps

comments.author: codex
comments.body: Fixed: PR #189 MERGED — opaque 'quarantined by collusion forensics' on the wire (session.ended + receipt); detailed heuristic reason preserved in durable quarantine EventLog row + server log.warning for operator audit. _check_collusion unchanged (unit test green). Full arena suite 189 passed. Same D7 class as #186.

cards.fix: Collapse quarantine_reason to an opaque/coarse value on the wire (or omit it); log the detailed forensic reason server-side only (log.warning), mirroring _opaque_error. Keep the EventLog row detailed for audit.
cards.impact: _check_collusion returns detailed reason strings (thresholds, 'repeatedly clicked choice: move 0', 'win-transfer W-L over N matches') into session.ended.quarantine_reason, surfaced to the visiting agent on the battle receipt. Leaking the exact heuristic enables trivial evasion (add 1 random move; stay under the 5-match win-transfer threshold) — same D7 anti-enumeration class as the battle_id leak (#186).
cards.title: Collusion quarantine_reason leaks heuristic internals to the agent (D7)

cards.fix: Append the dispute event AFTER a successful re-sim, OR guard with an idempotence check (skip if a dispute event for this battle_id already exists). Mirror the append-before-publish/fail-closed contract.
cards.impact: POST /battle/{id}/dispute appends the 'dispute' event (gateway.py:1771-1777) BEFORE running replay_input_log (:1790). If re-sim throws (:1822) the handler 500s but the dispute event is already durable; a client retry appends a SECOND dispute event — structural event-log corruption (violates Class A write-then-log intent for disputes).
cards.title: Dispute event appended BEFORE re-sim — duplicate events on retry

cards.fix: Capture before_rating AFTER the append_many (or serialize _finish per visitor_name with a per-player lock) so before/after bracket exactly one battle's mutation.
cards.impact: _finish captures before_rating (gateway.py:1131) OUTSIDE the atomic append window, then recomputes after (:1169). If a second concurrent _finish for the same visitor_name appends between A's capture and A's recompute, A's published_delta double-counts B's battle — a fairness/rating-integrity violation under async concurrency.
cards.title: Ladder published_delta race: concurrent _finish for same player inflates delta

cards.fix: Emit a durable quota_spent event on each spend_quota increment; replay quota_spent (scoped to current UTC day) on boot to rehydrate quota_used.
cards.impact: quota_used is in-memory only (consent.py:159-182); EventLog replay on boot (gateway.py:536-558) hydrates 'register'/'membership_grant' but NOT quota. A gateway restart resets every agent's daily rated cap, allowing >5 rated battles/UTC-day across restarts (anti-pay-to-rank-adjacent, ADR-0011 §3a/§5e). Exploitability is low on a stable single-process deploy (agent cannot force a restart), hence P2 not P1.
cards.title: Rated-battle quota not persisted — resets on gateway restart

comments.author: codex
comments.body: Fixed: PR #190 MERGED — scratchpad fenced between --- BEGIN/END NOTES --- in render_state so forged section headers can't masquerade as server-authored state. Note: scratchpad was already capped at MAX_SCRATCHPAD_CHARS=1200 (the 'unbounded' half of the audit note didn't apply). render_state+bridge suites green.

evidence: showdown_battle_bridge.py:~107 (scratchpad rendered into state); no escaping/validation/tests. adversarially confirmed (dogfood audit 2026-06-17)
cards.fix: Escape/segregate scratchpad when rendering (fence it in a clearly-delimited block, strip markdown headers, or render structured fields instead of raw markdown). Add a render test asserting injected headers don't duplicate real section headers.
cards.impact: Agent-authored scratchpad text is rendered into the battle-state string (showdown_battle_bridge.py:~107 / state render) without escaping, so an agent can inject a '## Recent turns'-style header + forged 'T#: ...' lines that appear in its OWN rendered state and poison its decision loop. Self-harm only today (no cross-agent/rating impact) → P2; but it is the latent cross-surface injection risk if scratchpad ever reaches a replay/opponent view.
cards.title: Scratchpad rendered into battle state without escaping — self-injection of fake turn lines

cards.fix: Bounded LRU/OrderedDict (cap N most-recent) for sessions+replays, or a TTL sweep that evicts ended sessions; replays should fall back to the durable EventLog/store rather than living in RAM forever.
cards.impact: ArenaGateway.sessions and .replays (gateway.py:564/567) grow ~1KB/session + ~2KB/replay with zero eviction (no del/pop/clear anywhere; _expire_if_stale only marks ended, doesn't remove). A long-running deploy OOMs after enough battles. Low urgency on a nano deploy that restarts often → P2.
cards.title: Unbounded sessions/replays dicts — eventual OOM on long-running deploy

comments.author: adx-cli
comments.body: Battle-UX foundation merged: PR #200 (typed line-protocol) + #201 (full protocol-log capture, byte-identical re-sim). Side quest: drained the SidecarPool review cascade (#197/#198/#203/#204) as 6 tiny PRs #202-#207 — fixes the pool capacity-leak + routing bugs relevant to ADX-ONLINE-002's 100-user push. Next: adx-client state-reducer, then {reason,action} + spectator/TUI/replay.

cards.fix: Ship the digest 2026-06-17 P1->P3 backlog as tiny PRs. P1 protocol foundation MERGED today (PR #200 typed lineproto, PR #201 full protocol-log capture + (seed,inputLog) re-sim parity). Next: state-reducer (client.py), {reason,action} schema + |-reasoning|, then TUI/spectator/replay.
cards.impact: Getting agentdex ONLINE means a watchable arena — the whole pitch is spectating agents fight. Without the typed protocol + state-reducer + spectator/replay, the online arena is unwatchable (raw |move| rows, no fog-of-war, no replay).
cards.title: launch: watchable Human-vs-AI battle UX (line-protocol + sim/client/view + spectator/TUI/replay)

cards.fix: Complete the agentdex-100-user-readiness assessment (in progress, 54/71 agents) -> capacity + integrity + security punch-list + go/no-go. Coordinate fixes with adx-cli; P0/P1 integrity items (atomic receipts ADX-P0-001) gate launch.
cards.impact: Getting agentdex online today requires a measured readiness verdict across the user-facing surface (capacity, integrity, security). This is the launch go/no-go gate the UX rides on top of.
cards.title: launch gate: agentdex 100-user readiness assessment + go/no-go

cards.fix: Add site/blog/ index + post template + nav link (EN+zh) and build-docs.py blog-page generation; rebase on og in-flight build-docs.py changes, do not clobber; render-verify.
cards.impact: No blog exists yet; the WHY/WHAT/HOW narrative needs a home. Render lane (bene).
cards.title: Scaffold the new /blog section on bene-site (index + post template + nav)

comments.author: bene-core
comments.body: Drafted + shipped as PR #14 (good-night-oppie/bene, branch content/blog-why-bene). Abstract-only, leak-scan clean. Source md only; render is bene's lane (BENE-DOC-01 scaffold + BENE-DOC-10 deploy).

cards.fix: Write blog/why-bene.md (EN). ABSTRACT ONLY: pseudo-code, NO agentdex-cli internals. zh routed to og.
cards.impact: Frames the whole site: BENE is the durable, auditable local-first multi-agent substrate that backs agentdex-cli (the arena). Gom-jabbar harness thesis.
cards.title: Blog post: WHY we build BENE — the harness behind the arena

cards.fix: Write blog/what-is-bene.md (EN); each pillar with one REAL example snippet from examples/, ground-truthed against the CLI/code.
cards.impact: Crisp WHAT: per-agent VFS, checkpoints, engrams, eval-probe kill-gates, autonomy ladder, MCP server, evolutionary meta-harness search.
cards.title: Blog post: WHAT BENE is — the seven pillars

cards.fix: Write blog/how-we-build-bene.md (EN), grounded in the real repo (probes, mh search, tiny-PR discipline).
cards.impact: Answers HOW: tiny PRs, falsifiable eval-probe kill-gates, the breeding program, trace-based RAG.
cards.title: Blog post: HOW we build BENE — harness engineering + eval-gated evolution

cards.fix: Expand docs (architecture/quickstart/pillar pages) with runnable, explained snippets drawn from examples/*.py; each ground-truth-run exit 0.
cards.impact: Mission asks for REAL examples; 19 example scripts exist but are not woven into the docs narrative.
cards.title: Surface real runnable examples per pillar in the docs

cards.fix: Write docs/case-studies/cs03-multi-agent-arena.md. HARD CONSTRAINT: agentdex-cli is the real consumer but DO NOT expose internals — pseudo-code/abstract ONLY.
cards.impact: Flagship case study and the concrete WHY: BENE as the substrate backing a competitive multi-agent coding platform.
cards.title: Case study: multi-agent coding arena on BENE (ABSTRACT)

cards.fix: Write docs/case-studies/cs04-trace-rag-other-memory.md grounded in the real engram/retrieve CLI.
cards.impact: Shows engrams + trace-based RAG — the next agent never starts cold.
cards.title: Case study: trace-based RAG / Other Memory (engrams)

cards.fix: Write docs/case-studies/cs05-meta-harness-evolution.md grounded in the mh CLI + eval probes.
cards.impact: Shows the breeding program — kill-gated promotion of evolved harness strategies.
cards.title: Case study: evolutionary meta-harness search

cards.fix: Author mermaid diagrams for the single-file Nexus, engram compression ladder (tiers 0-4), autonomy ladder L0-L4; embed in docs (final HTML render routed to bene).
cards.impact: Mission asks for designs; visual diagrams make the architecture legible.
cards.title: Design: architecture diagrams (Nexus, engram ladder, autonomy ladder)

cards.fix: Regen HTML via build-docs.py, sync the 4-copy mirror chain, Koyeb deploy, render-verify all 4 view x lang per the bilingual-render lesson; coordinate with og translation pass.
cards.impact: New content must reach the live site; recurring render/deploy lane (bene).
cards.title: Render + deploy all new blog/docs/case-study/design content

evidence: __main__.py:128 hardcodes _file_inbox_notifier to container /tmp; __main__.py:7 docstring promises ARENA_OWNER_WEBHOOK but no code reads it; gateway.py:622 notify_owner is the only path.
cards.fix: Add an env-selected notifier (ARENA_OWNER_WEBHOOK / email) in build_gateway, keep file-inbox as fallback; wire into notify_owner.
cards.impact: Self-serve /enroll/confirm is unreachable for external users -> 0 tokens minted -> no players can onboard.
cards.title: Wire a real confirmation-code delivery channel (env webhook/email, file fallback) into build_gateway()

evidence: consent.py:136 mint() works; gateway.py:639-649 ConsentClaims shape; _registered append-only gateway.py:637.
cards.fix: One-shot script using live persistent ARENA_SIGNING_KEY_HEX; append register events so names survive restart.
cards.impact: Fastest path to real players today: hand pre-minted tokens to configured agents without the broken confirm flow.
cards.title: Batch-mint N consent tokens via one-shot script (emit register events) + distribute, bypassing self-serve confirm

evidence: gateway.py:599-626 stores pending_enrollments[code] but never returns it; arena_play.py:61-77 only works co-located.
cards.fix: Return pending code only when ARENA_ENROLL_RETURN_CODE set; keep test_mcp_surface.py A1 green when unset.
cards.impact: Self-service onboarding alternative to batch-mint for a curated launch; default-off preserves A1 out-of-band invariant.
cards.title: Env-gated playtest enroll path (ARENA_ENROLL_RETURN_CODE, off by default) that returns the code

evidence: arena_play.py:10 promise out-of-band delivery with no code.
cards.fix: Correct the docs to the real (curated/batch-mint) onboarding path; ties to ENROLL-P0-delivery-channel. Overlaps ADX-P2-001 (done).
cards.impact: SKILL.md/bootstrap.sh/ENROLLMENT.md dead-end users at pending_owner_confirmation with no real channel.
cards.title: Fix docs that promise a prod webhook/email delivery channel that does not exist

evidence: gateway.py:1818 replay_input_log(battle_id=f'{id}-dispute') never start-ed; pool.py:95-98 raises 'not owned'.
cards.fix: Start the dispute battle on a pool member before replay, or special-case replay/import as battle-less; add test_pool replay test.
cards.impact: Disputes 500 the instant ADX_SIDECAR_POOL_SIZE>1 (the config 100 concurrent needs) -> broken integrity surface under scale.
cards.title: Route dispute replay op through SidecarPool (start <id>-dispute or special-case replay as battle-less) + regression test

evidence: sidecar.py:117-126 fails pending futures with no respawn; pool.py:96-106 keeps dead sidecar in _owner/_load.
cards.fix: On Node death, respawn the pool member and purge its _owner/_load entries so _least_loaded stops routing to it.
cards.impact: One OOM permanently degrades a fraction of 100 users: dead sidecar stays in routing map, new battles routed to the corpse.
cards.title: Auto-respawn a dead sidecar in SidecarPool and evict its battle_id/_load routes

evidence: grep retry-after=0; self.sessions keyed by battle_id only (gateway.py:572); BattleSession.claims_token_id gateway.py:388.
cards.fix: Add Retry-After header to the capacity 503; cap concurrent battles per claims_token_id. ADR-0012 sec7. (bounded queue deferred.)
cards.impact: Bare 503 gives clients no backoff signal; one owner can fill the whole pool and starve the other 99 users.
cards.title: Admission control: Retry-After on capacity 503 + per-owner concurrent-battle cap (anti-monopolization)

evidence: gateway.py:1406/1418-1420 static _ARENA_HEALTH; Sidecar.rss_mb adx_showdown/sidecar.py:153; SidecarPool.rss_mb pool.py:108.
cards.fix: Compute readiness from app.state.sidecar rss_mb + returncode (cheap one-ps-read/sidecar); fail when unhealthy.
cards.impact: Platform never recycles a sick container -> an OOM/dead-sidecar spiral stays 'healthy' and keeps taking traffic.
cards.title: Make /healthz a real readiness probe (sidecar alive + RSS) instead of static {ok:true}

evidence: no /metrics/counters (grep none); len(self.sessions) gateway.py:572 + rss_mb already exist.
cards.fix: Expose len(self.sessions) + rss_mb + a 503 counter via a small /metrics endpoint.
cards.impact: Operator is blind between a healthy spike and an OOM spiral; the 503 path increments no counter.
cards.title: Add /metrics (or /debug/stats): RSS, active battles, 503 count (queue depth when queue lands)

cards.fix: Maintain a live in-memory ratings fold updated per battle-result; serve /ladder from cache; preserve Q5 anti-pay-to-rank parity.
cards.impact: recompute_ladder is 3 full O(N) passes run synchronously on the single event loop per /ladder, twice per rated finish, whoami, badge.
cards.title: Incremental ladder fold + cached /ladder (full recompute = boot/repair only); coordinate with ADX-P1-007

evidence: self.sessions={} on boot gateway.py:572; boot replay rebuilds only names/memberships gateway.py:544-565; /choose 403 gateway.py:1663-1664.
cards.fix: Detect begin-without-end on boot and return 409 interrupted; full (seed,inputLog) resurrection needs a WAL schema change (backlog).
cards.impact: After a restart, /choose on a dropped in-flight battle returns an opaque 403, confusing players mid-game.
cards.title: Return a clear 409 'interrupted by restart' for begin-without-end battles after gateway restart (vs opaque 403)

evidence: engine primitive exists (pokemon-showdown sim/state.js:79/99) but sidecar.mjs has no snapshot/restore op.
cards.fix: Add snapshot/restore ops to sidecar.mjs on top of the existing engine primitive + WAL replay rail.
cards.impact: Post-launch hardening: today a sidecar crash loses in-flight battles (load test shows crash isn't the demonstrated failure mode yet).
cards.title: Wire snapshot/restore ops into sidecar.mjs using engine State.serializeBattle (full in-flight crash recovery)

cards.fix: Add a derived ratings view to eventsync's PG mirror for replica reads.
cards.impact: Only load-bearing once we go multi-core/horizontal; single-process today rebuilds from disk on boot.
cards.title: Add a ratings projection table to the PG mirror for multi-instance/replica reads (post-MVP)

evidence: cli.py:821 forwards only k.startswith('ARENA_'); __main__.py:179 reads ADX_SIDECAR_POOL_SIZE; sidecar.py:76 reads OLD_SPACE_MB.
cards.fix: Extend cli.py env-forward allowlist to ADX_SIDECAR_* (pays off on the multi-core box).
cards.impact: The SidecarPool scale lever is silently dropped on deploy -> pool size can't be set on the bigger box without the --env-vars escape hatch.
cards.title: Forward ADX_SIDECAR_POOL_SIZE + ADX_SIDECAR_MAX_OLD_SPACE_MB in `adx deploy` (only ARENA_* forwarded today)

evidence: 126 names LLM tier first; loadtest doc:61-63 defers it; arena server makes $0 LLM calls (bots.py:1) so risk is 100 client agents.
cards.fix: Probe /chat/completions concurrency + read /v1/usage/summary budget headroom on the shared AI_BUILDER_TOKEN proxy.
cards.impact: LLM proxy is the EXPECTED #1 bottleneck and is unmeasured -> go/no-go for 100 concurrent is unknown.
cards.title: Run ADR-0012 MUST-MEASURE #3: LLM fan-out vs platform proxy rate/budget at 100 concurrent

evidence: events.py:72-97 single global fcntl lock + single fd; per-turn append hot path gateway.py:1702; loadtest covered sim only.
cards.fix: Microbench events.py append under concurrent turns; WAL-SQLite migration is the to-build fix if it's a ceiling.
cards.impact: Diagnostic: tells whether the single global append lock is a real ceiling at 100 concurrent.
cards.title: Run ADR-0012 MUST-MEASURE #2: EventLog append throughput at ~100x turns/s (single fcntl-lock NDJSON)

evidence: __main__.py:61-67 mints ephemeral key with warning-only when unset; consent.py:118-120 fail-closes only if BOTH empty.
cards.fix: Confirm ARENA_SIGNING_KEY_HEX is set in the live deploy env_vars (or enroll->restart->whoami probe). Overlaps ADX-P2-004.
cards.impact: If the key is the ephemeral fallback, every consent token is invalidated on sleep/wake -> all players logged out mid-launch.
cards.title: Verify the live deploy has a persistent ARENA_SIGNING_KEY_HEX (else all tokens die on sleep/wake/redeploy)

evidence: ADR-0012:117-118; deploy payload cli.py:844-849 has no instance-size/scale-to-zero field; quota 2/2 used.
cards.fix: Instructor provisions a larger always-on SKU (deploy API has no instance-size field) or confirm none exists -> external host.
cards.impact: THE hard blocker for true 100 concurrent: 256MB nano physically cannot host gateway + multi-sidecar pool.
cards.title: INSTRUCTOR: provision a multi-core instance bigger than the 256MB nano, scale-to-zero DISABLED

evidence: no self-serve DELETE (go/no-go:38-42); Dockerfile:60 shell-form CMD honoring $PORT.
cards.fix: Instructor DELETE meta-vex (green-lit 2026-06-11), or operator stands up VPS + DNS + ~17 env vars + secrets (Dockerfile is portable).
cards.impact: Contingency if no on-platform bigger SKU: need a service slot or an off-platform host for the scale instance.
cards.title: INSTRUCTOR/OPERATOR: free the 2/2 quota (delete meta-vex, user green-lit) OR stand up external ~$5/mo VPS fallback

evidence: docs/runbooks/ holds only badge-admin.md + membership-admin.md; defaults POOL_SIZE=1
cards.fix: Consolidate into docs/runbooks/arena-go-live.md incl the concrete bigger-instance env set (<=20 cap; inventory ~17).
cards.impact: Dangerous defaults + boot-crash envs + keep-alive + rollback are scattered across go-nogo + loadtest + ADR-0012.
cards.title: Write a go-live deploy/scale/rollback RUNBOOK (pre-flight envs, thresholds, rollback)
